_note: Rules live in the daemon's `rules` Qdrant collection (host-bind state/qdrant/storage), NOT in git — so a volume loss or tenant change wipes them. This file is the versioned source of truth to RE-APPLY them.

Re-apply: for each entry, call the MCP `rules` tool with action=add, passing label/title/content/priority/scope/tags and your repo cwd (so project-scoped rules bind to the right tenant_id). See docs/runbooks/restore-rules-and-global-ignore.md.

These are PROJECT-scoped rules only. 7 canonical GLOBAL tool-usage rules also exist (search-first@100, grep-for-exact, scope-project, branch-default, no-proj-write, confirm-mut, agent-branch) — see them with `rules action=list scope=global`; they are NOT captured here.
Keep this file in sync whenever you add/change a behavioral rule. Last synced: 2026-05-29 (removed duplicate project search-first; the global @100 is canonical).

rules.label: look-then-write
rules.title: Inspect daemon shared state before overwriting it
rules.content: Before overwriting or deleting daemon-owned shared state (global.wqmignore, named volumes, the queue/search SQLite DBs), inspect/diff the target first. The live global.wqmignore is NOT empty — it carries custom entries (e.g. `**/bws-dev-plataform/testlink/**`, `**/zabbix/zabbix/**`) that a blind truncate-overwrite would destroy. Prefer append over overwrite; diff against assets/global.wqmignore.example rather than replacing.

rules.label: daemon-via-grpc
rules.title: Operate the daemon via container gRPC, not host wqm
rules.content: Reach the daemon through the dockerized stack — MCP gRPC, or `docker exec wqm-memexd wqm ...` for CLI ops (e.g. `wqm admin requeue-failed`). Do NOT run the host `wqm` CLI: it resolves the wrong DB (host XDG path, not the daemon's volume). Running wqm INSIDE wqm-memexd resolves the real /var/lib/memexd/memexd.db.

rules.label: db-via-sidecar
rules.title: Query daemon SQLite via sidecar on the active volume
rules.content: Inspect/query the daemon's SQLite (memexd.db, queue/state) with a sidecar container mounting volume `workspace-qdrant-mcp_memexd_db` (RW + WAL = safe concurrent read with the live daemon). Gotchas: there are multiple memexd volumes — the compose-prefixed `workspace-qdrant-mcp_memexd_db` is live; bare `memexd_db` was a stale empty orphan (removed). The live Qdrant index is a HOST BIND at state/qdrant/storage (no qdrant volume). global.wqmignore is a host bind at state/memexd/, not inside the DB volume.

rules.label: validate-build
rules.title: Validate the crate, then rebuild the container image
rules.content: For Rust daemon changes, validate the specific crate FIRST on Windows — `cargo check/clippy/test -p workspace-qdrant-core` (or -grpc); core/grpc compile without ORT. Then deploy by rebuilding the image: `docker compose build memexd && docker compose up -d memexd` (memexd is heavy; mcp is a fast TS rebuild). The running container keeps the OLD binary until rebuilt — source edits alone do nothing.

rules.label: wqmignore-edit
rules.title: Edit global.wqmignore at host bind; use **/ dir prefixes
rules.content: The daemon's global ignore is the host-bind file state/memexd/global.wqmignore (read at reconcile; mounted at /var/lib/memexd/global.wqmignore in-container). APPEND, never truncate — it holds custom + worktree patterns. Directory patterns MUST use the `**/` prefix: the Rust `ignore` crate's add_ignore() anchors patterns at the file's parent dir, so `.claude/` alone won't match project-level (use `**/.claude/`). Worktrees and .claude/.codex/.agents are excluded by design (indexing them = ~98% noise). Canonical template: assets/global.wqmignore.example.

rules.label: vitest-scoped
rules.title: Run vitest scoped to one file via cd + npx
rules.content: Run a single MCP-server test with `cd src/typescript/mcp-server && npx vitest run <file-substring>`. Do NOT use `npm --prefix ... exec -- vitest --root ...` — the positional path filter is dropped and the entire ~859-test suite runs (and crashes on the server-http-transport teardown with a stack-overflow). Typecheck with `npx tsc --noEmit` from the mcp-server dir.

rules.label: mcp-pass-cwd
rules.title: Always pass cwd (or projectId) to workspace-qdrant tools
rules.content: The MCP server runs over HTTP (dockerized) and CANNOT observe your working directory. Always pass `cwd` (absolute path) — or an explicit `projectId` — to `search`, `grep`, `list`, `retrieve`, and `rules`. Omitting both yields "Could not detect project" (the Windows/9P mount makes path-based detection fail: client sends C:\..., daemon registered /run/desktop/mnt/host/c/...).

rules.label: worktree-tools
rules.title: On worktrees: local Read/Grep; MCP search against main
rules.content: Git worktrees (.claude/worktrees/, parallel agent worktrees) are NOT indexed by design (excluded in global.wqmignore — indexing them is ~98% noise). So inside a worktree, read its own files with the native Read/Grep tools, and use workspace-qdrant `search`/`grep` (MCP) only against the main canonical index. Merging back to main re-indexes via the file watcher.
